# Derived Concept Guide

How to write well-formed `.derived` files.

## Structure

```
derived Name [T] {
  purpose { ... }
  composes { Concept [T], derived OtherDerived [T] }
  syncs { required: [sync-a, sync-b] }
  surface {
    action name(params) { matches: Concept/action(field: "value") }
    query name(params) -> Concept/action(args)
  }
  principle { after ... then ... }
}
```

## Surface Actions

Two kinds of matches:
- Field matching: `matches: Concept/action(field: "value")`
- derivedContext matching: `matches: derivedContext "DerivedName/action"`
- Entry + triggers: `entry: Concept/action` + `triggers: [Concept/action(arg: ?binding)]`

## Surface Queries

Two forms:
- Inline: `query name(params) -> Concept/action(args)`
- Block: `query name(params) { reads: Concept/action(arg: ?binding) }`

## Principle Block

```
principle {
  after surfaceAction(arg: value)
  then Concept/action(arg: value) -> ok
  and  OtherConcept/action(arg: value) -> ok
}
```

## When to Use Derived Concepts

Use when a composition produces a user-facing abstraction with its
own name and purpose (Trash, Registration, TaskBoard).

Don't use when grouping is purely organizational (use a suite) or
when the abstraction has independent state (create a concept).
